stonetop.summary: The Dread River is the eastern border of the World’s End.

text.content: The Dread River is the eastern border of the World’s End. It flows down from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.5vFbBIn18TKtCF91]{the Whitefang Mountains}, through the North and @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.apczJSWiu0M5dFrF]{South Manmarch} (pages 282 and 350), fed by numerous creeks and streams along the way.

Always

  • From afar: a sense of stillness
  • As you get closer: the rush of the current
  • The far shore: ruin-dotted, lonely, mysterious
  • Near the water: an uneasy feeling, like anything could be in there— watching, waiting, hungry

Spring

  • The river swollen and flooded from snowmelt, spilling out over its banks
  • Swarms of flies, like clouds of smoke
  • The smell of mud and rotting fish

Summer

  • Brilliant green along the shores, sunlight sparkling on water
  • Endless chorus of waterfowl, starlings, insects, current
  • The occasional splash—just a fish, or a spooked turtle, maybe?

In its lower reach, the river itself is wide and deep, never less than a quarter-mile across and dozens—if not hundreds—of feet deep. From afar, it appears placid and still. But it flows with a terrible and relentless current.

The western shore alternates between wetlands, wide-open flood plains, and rugged bluffs, ruins scattered all along its length. It would be an idyllic place to live, except that the length of the river is cursed.

Autumn

  • Riot of autumn colors along the shores, reflected in the water
  • Blankets of mist, hiding the river but amplifying its rush and gurgle
  • Honk and flap of waterfowl flying south, or wheeling overhead before alighting

Winter

  • White-capped trees and bluffs and slopes
  • Ice growing out from the shores
  • Slate grey water, more menacing than ever with the promise of deadly cold

• What famous treasure is said to lie in the Ruins at Road’s End?
  • What once drove you to spend a night encamped along the Dread River? What evil did you encounter?
  • Who do you know who once saw the Suileach, the Wurm with Two-Hundred Eyes? How did it change them?
  • Aside from empty ruins, what is noticeably strange about the lands across the river?
  • Does one cross the Dread River en route to @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.DPUvs6Ss8UHXEsCJ]{Lygos}? If so, how? And what separates the southern lands from those east of the river? Mountains? A desert? A sea?

• Bandits or cultists take up residence in ruins on the Dread River’s shores.
  • The PCs acquire a cursed treasure, originally looted from the Ruins at Road’s End.
  • Some fool is mounting an expedition to cross the Dread River.

People from east of the river land on the western shore.

The Highway runs from @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.VZ3YkXn70E1C7TEO]{Barrier Pass} through @UUID[Compendium.stonetop_pwd.stonetop-locations.JournalEntry.uXlyry9CpXUz4ooR]{Marshedge} and ends at the Dread River. Where the road ends, there was once a sizeable city, a bustling hub of trade and learning.

The Tempest Lords called this city “Northernmost,” for it was founded as their most far-flung outpost. The Stone Lords called it Aavonkaer (“River City”). To the Green Lords it was Pazara (“The Marketplace”). The Rime Lords called it Jalampravahati (“Through Where the Waters Flow”).

Now, though, the city is called the Ruins at Road’s End. For when the Stone Lords’ servants rebelled and woke the Things Below, the Dread River belched forth countless horrors. The city’s inhabitants were slaughtered in a single night.

Throughout the time of the Barrow- Builders, various warlords and would-be sorcerer-kings claimed the ruined city. They sought to recover the lost artifacts of the Makers, or to harness the city’s restless souls, or to bend the horrors in the river to their will. Sometimes, they even succeeded. But never for very long.

The ruins sprawl for miles along the shore, and similar vestiges of the past are visible across the river. The legs of a once-mighty bridge still poke out from the water. Everyone agrees that great wealth and troves of lore remain among the crumbling walls. But everyone also agrees that those old cellar holes and skeletal structures—along with anything dug out of them—are cursed.

Beyond the Dread River lie more ruins, bluffs and woods, and gently sloping plains. The Manmarchers migrated from east of the Dread River sometime before the Time of Cataclysm. But if people dwell there still, they keep well away from the shore. And if any have ventured across the river and returned, they’re keeping quiet about it. Who or what lies east of the Dread River? That’s for you and your group to decide, but perhaps one might find…

  • … the husk of a sprawling empire, home now to only jackals and wolves; … nomadic raiders, bloody-handed even by the Manmarchers’ standards; … descendants of the Makers, greatly diminished, in isolated fiefs … a people in thrall to an undying sorcerer-king; … a land of horrors, thoroughly tainted by the Things Below; … a barren land, dead and still, bereft of spirits and magic; … the Forest Folk; … some combination of the above; or … something else entirely.

Pick 1 or combine 2, or have someone roll the Die of Fate (d12).

RollResult
1Open water, who knows how deep?
2Rocks/eddies/rapids
3Waterfall/smaller stream feeding the river
4Swamp/drowned trees/logjam
5–6Shallows/marsh/mud/reeds
7–8Sandbar/beach/shingle/floodplain
9Slope/hill
10Bluff/promontory/rocky outcrop
11Caves
12Island

If you like, roll 1d6 for size/scope/intensity.

For any of these, pick 1 or have someone roll the Die of Fate.

1d6 discovery

RollResult
1Change of terrain and roll again
2A chance for insight into a threat or danger (e.g. tracks that reveal numbers, whereabouts, etc.)
3Useful or valuable flora
4encounter (see below)
5–6point of interest

1d6 encounter

RollResult
1–2Big and dangerous beast, easily provoked: aurochs, boar, a cave bear, a thunder drake, a horned drake, etc.
3–4Game, mostly harmless and prone to bolt: deer, water fowl, rabbits, etc.
5People, exploring, in trouble, or hiding out
6Spirit of the wild, curious or indifferent

1d6 point of interest

RollResult
1A tomb, site, or artifact of the Barrow Builders
2A site sacred to the spirits of the wild
3–4A ruin or fortification of the Stone Lords
5A ruin, site, or artifact of the Tempest Lords
6A ruin or artifact of some other group of Makers

For any point of interest, decide or roll to see what taints it (if anything).

1d6 what taints it

RollResult
1–2The Things Below
3–4Death and the undying
5Both of the above
6Nothing, strangely

Hazards

  • A stretch of water, and reason to brave it
  • A bluff to climb and a reason to climb it
  • Rockslides or mudslides
  • The river’s current, surprisingly strong
  • Poor footing (mud, silt, branches, dropoffs) hidden by water/reeds
  • Winter ice, thinner than it looks
  • Swarms of midges or mosquitos— annoying, distracting, painful, diseased
  • Starlings—obscuring, drowning out sound, buffeting, possibly host to a grwgnach

People

  • Bandits, hiding among the ruins, possibly cursed/corrupted
  • Cultists, led by a sorcerer
  • Manmarchers: outcasts or foolhardy explorers
  • Adventurers or antiquarians
  • A hdour or necromancer, trawling the ruins for power
